title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
limitations: single-step retrieval, no abstention, no citations
relevance_to_auralynq: foundational RAG framing for the paper's related work

title: Benchmarking LLMs in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RGB)
limitations: focuses on 4 abilities; no calibration metrics, no trace

title: RAGAS: Automated Evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation
relevance_to_auralynq: reused as a generation-quality signal in Auralynq metrics
possible_gap: no abstention/calibration/trace metrics; judge reliability untested here

title: ARES: An Automated Evaluation Framework for RAG
metrics: context relevance, answer faithfulness, answer relevance (PPI)
limitations: requires judge fine-tuning + human PPI anchors
relevance_to_auralynq: judge-reliability angle informs our evaluator-agreement study
possible_gap: does not evaluate abstention calibration or agent traces

title: CRAG -- Comprehensive RAG Benchmark
task: factual QA w/ web+KG mock APIs
limitations: web/KG simulated via mock APIs; English only
relevance_to_auralynq: closest to corpus-vs-web routing eval; reusable QA + abstention labels
possible_gap: no trace-feature analysis; no open-model local-first study

title: RAGBench: Explainable Benchmark for RAG Systems (TRACe)
limitations: finetuned RoBERTa beats LLM judges; no agentic loop
relevance_to_auralynq: TRACe adherence/completeness map onto our sufficiency features
possible_gap: no abstention decision/calibration; no trace observability

title: MIRAGE-Bench: Multilingual RAG Evaluation Arena
relevance_to_auralynq: multilingual + heuristic-feature judge informs our trace-feature study
possible_gap: no calibrated abstention; no agent trace logging

title: GroUSE: A Benchmark to Evaluate Evaluators in Grounded QA
limitations: meta-eval of judges, not systems; small
possible_gap: no end-to-end agentic pipeline; no local-first models

title: FaithBench: Diverse Hallucination Benchmark for Summarization
limitations: summarization not doc-grounded QA; detectors ~50% acc
relevance_to_auralynq: hallucination-risk labeling methodology informs our risk score

title: Enabling LLMs to Generate Text with Citations (ALCE)
possible_gap: no abstention calibration; no agent trace; no local-first

title: GraphRAG: From Local to Global Query-Focused Summarization
relevance_to_auralynq: graph-expansion baseline for our retrieval ablation
possible_gap: no abstention/calibration; no per-query trace metrics

title: PathRAG: Pruning Graph-based RAG with Relational Paths
relevance_to_auralynq: Auralynq's graph retriever is PathRAG-style; core baseline
possible_gap: graph-path support as a sufficiency feature is unexplored

title: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
relevance_to_auralynq: frames Auralynq within agentic RAG taxonomy
possible_gap: calls out lack of standardized agentic-trace evaluation

title: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
limitations: chat not grounded QA; known judge biases
relevance_to_auralynq: grounds our caution that LLM judges are not ground truth

title: Know Your Limits: A Survey of Abstention in LLMs
relevance_to_auralynq: organizes abstention metrics we implement (Abstain-ECE etc.)

title: On Calibration of Modern Neural Networks
relevance_to_auralynq: source of ECE/temperature-scaling we adapt to abstention
possible_gap: calibration of RAG sufficiency scores not addressed

title: Do LLMs Know When to NOT Answer? Investigating Abstention
